extraction.one_line_summary: DENIS Rohan drove BMC to TTT victory in 13.7km technical Nîmes circuit, repeating their 2015 success on this same course.
tactical_patterns: BMC's specific experience advantage: won this exact Nîmes TTT course in 2015, suggesting course-specific reconnaissance pays dividends.
Quick-Step set early 16:04 benchmark that only one team could beat, showing depth threshold for sub-16-minute technical TTTs.
Sky slowed deliberately before finish to wait for fifth rider, indicating even top TTT teams miscalculate threshold timing.

rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Astana lost over 30 seconds in opening TTT, revealing preparation issues before GC battle begins
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Trek-Segafredo finished 30+ seconds down in his final Vuelta, weak TTT support for farewell tour
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Sky third despite defending TTT title, suggesting either tactical conservatism or reduced depth from 2016
rider_signals.ATAPUMA Darwin: negative: Crashed in first bend showing technical vulnerability on urban circuits under pressure
rider_signals.CHAVEZ Esteban: positive: Orica-Scott fifth at 17 seconds despite not being TTT specialists, strong support depth for GC
